statement: Chinese AI labs (Zhipu AI, DeepSeek) are pursuing custom AI/inference ASICs to reduce reliance on Nvidia (and Huawei) GPUs, though viable in-house silicon is estimated to be more than two years out.
why: A broadening shift by top Chinese AI labs toward proprietary silicon, driven by surging inference demand and tightening export controls, points to long-run erosion of Nvidia's addressable China GPU demand and rising custom-ASIC competition.

impact.mechanism: Custom in-house ASICs are an alternative to merchant GPUs; more Chinese labs building them narrows Nvidia's China customer base while growing alternative silicon supply.

evidence.excerpt: DeepSeek is separately reported to be developing an in-house inference processor explicitly aimed at reducing reliance on BOTH Nvidia and Huawei hardware.

confidence.basis: Secondary coverage of one story (DigiTimes headline plus BigGo Finance and tech360tv corroboration); forward-looking, no partner selected, ASIC 2+ years out.
